description: Enhance learn:plan with Profile-aware personalization by improving Agent prompt, Gap Analysis, resource scoring, and learning path visualization
approach: Phase 4 builds on Phase 2's simplified workflow to add intelligent personalization features. The approach enhances the learn-planning-agent.md with Profile-Aware Planning Instructions that adjust difficulty based on experience level, skip high-proficiency topics, and build on existing knowledge. Gap Analysis is improved to detect related experience (transferable skills) and recommend focus areas. A resource scoring algorithm personalizes recommendations based on user experience level. Learning path visualization is added to User Confirmation for better understanding. This phase depends on Phase 2 (ISS-20260127-006) being completed first, as the simplified workflow provides a cleaner foundation for adding personalization logic.

tasks.title: Enhance learn-planning-agent.md with Profile-Aware instructions
tasks.description: Add comprehensive Profile-Aware Planning Instructions section to learn-planning-agent.md that teaches the agent how to personalize plans based on user profile (known_topics, proficiency levels, experience_level). Include rules for skipping high-proficiency topics, adjusting difficulty distribution, building on existing knowledge, and matching resource types to experience level.

modification_points.target: After Phase 1: Context Loading section (around line 75)
modification_points.change: Insert new Profile-Aware Planning Instructions section with 4 personalization rules

implementation: Read learn-planning-agent.md to identify insertion point after Phase 1: Context Loading
Insert new section: '#### Phase 1.5: Profile-Aware Analysis'
Add Profile Analysis subsection showing how to extract known_topics with proficiency levels

Add 4 Personalization Rules: (1) Skip High-Proficiency Topics (proficiency >= 0.8 → mark KP as optional), (2) Adjust Difficulty (beginner: more easy KPs, advanced: more hard KPs), (3) Build on Existing Knowledge (identify related topics as prerequisites), (4) Resource Recommendation (match resource types to experience level)
Add examples for each rule showing input profile and expected output behavior
Add note: 'These rules are applied during Phase 3: Knowledge Point Generation'
Reference implementation plan: ANL-learn-plan-optimization-2026-01-27 Phase 4.1

commands: cd C:\Project\Claude-Code-Workflow && node -e "const fs = require('fs'); const content = fs.readFileSync('.claude/agents/learn-planning-agent.md', 'utf8'); if (!content.includes('Profile-Aware') || !content.includes('Skip High-Proficiency')) { console.error('Profile-Aware instructions not added'); process.exit(1); }"

criteria: Profile-Aware Planning Instructions section added to agent prompt
4 personalization rules documented with clear logic
Examples provided for each rule showing expected behavior
Rules reference profile.known_topics, profile.proficiency, profile.experience_level
Integration point specified (Phase 3: Knowledge Point Generation)
verification: Read learn-planning-agent.md and verify new section exists
Check that all 4 rules are documented
Verify examples show input profile → output behavior
Confirm rules are actionable for LLM agent

commit.message_template:
feat(learn): add Profile-Aware planning instructions to agent

Enhance learn-planning-agent with 4 personalization rules:
- Skip high-proficiency topics (>= 0.8)
- Adjust difficulty by experience level
- Build on existing knowledge
- Match resources to experience level

Ref: ANL-learn-plan-optimization-2026-01-27 Phase 4.1

tasks.title: Improve Gap Analysis with related experience detection
tasks.description: Enhance the Gap Analysis function in plan.md (lines 225-256) to detect related experience (transferable skills) and provide more detailed analysis. Add skill mapping for common technology relationships (e.g., React → Vue, Python → Ruby) and calculate transferability scores. Add recommended focus areas based on gap patterns.

modification_points.change: Replace simplified Gap Analysis with enhanced version including related_experience detection

implementation: Read plan.md lines 225-256 to understand current Gap Analysis implementation
Expand gapAnalysis object to include: missing_topics, weak_topics, strong_topics, related_experience (new), recommended_focus (new)
Add relatedSkills mapping object with common technology relationships: react→[vue,angular,svelte], python→[ruby,javascript], typescript→[javascript,flow], etc.
Implement related experience detection: for each goalKeyword, check if user knows related skills from mapping
Calculate transferability score (default 0.7 for mapped relationships)
Add recommended_focus logic: if missing_topics > 5 → 'Start with fundamentals', if weak_topics > 0 → 'Strengthen weak areas first'
Update console output to show related experience: 'Related experience: React (from Vue, transferability: 0.7)'
Add comment referencing implementation plan Phase 4.2

commands: cd C:\Project\Claude-Code-Workflow && node -e "const fs = require('fs'); const content = fs.readFileSync('.claude/commands/learn/plan.md', 'utf8'); if (!content.includes('related_experience') || !content.includes('transferability')) { console.error('Related experience detection not added'); process.exit(1); }"

criteria: Gap Analysis expanded to include related_experience and recommended_focus fields
relatedSkills mapping added with at least 5 technology relationships
Transferability score calculated for related skills (0.0-1.0)
Recommended focus areas generated based on gap patterns
Console output shows related experience with transferability scores
verification: Test with profile knowing React, goal 'Learn Vue' → should detect related experience
Test with profile knowing Python, goal 'Learn Ruby' → should show transferability
Verify recommended_focus appears when missing_topics > 5
Check console output includes related experience section

commit.message_template:
feat(learn): enhance Gap Analysis with related experience detection

Add transferable skills detection and focus recommendations.
Maps common technology relationships (React→Vue, Python→Ruby).
Calculates transferability scores for better personalization.

Ref: ANL-learn-plan-optimization-2026-01-27 Phase 4.2

tasks.title: Add resource scoring algorithm to agent prompt
tasks.description: Add a Resource Scoring Algorithm section to learn-planning-agent.md that defines how to calculate personalization scores for resources based on quality tier, experience level match, and topic relevance. This helps the agent recommend the most appropriate resources for each user.

modification_points.target: After Phase 4: Resource Scoring section (around line 173)
modification_points.change: Insert Resource Scoring Algorithm with formula and experience_match matrix

implementation: Read learn-planning-agent.md to locate Phase 4: Resource Scoring section
After the quality rubric definition, add new subsection: 'Resource Scoring Algorithm'
Define scoring formula: score = base_quality * experience_match * topic_relevance
Add base_quality mapping: gold=1.0, silver=0.7, bronze=0.5
Add experience_match matrix: beginner (tutorials=1.0, docs=0.5, papers=0.2), intermediate (tutorials=0.7, docs=1.0, papers=0.5), advanced (tutorials=0.3, docs=0.8, papers=1.0)
Add topic_relevance: 1.0 if exact match, 0.7 if related, 0.5 if tangential
Add instruction: 'Recommend top 3-5 resources per KP, sorted by personalization score'
Add example calculation showing how score is computed for a beginner user
Reference implementation plan Phase 4.3

commands: cd C:\Project\Claude-Code-Workflow && node -e "const fs = require('fs'); const content = fs.readFileSync('.claude/agents/learn-planning-agent.md', 'utf8'); if (!content.includes('Resource Scoring Algorithm') || !content.includes('experience_match')) { console.error('Resource scoring algorithm not added'); process.exit(1); }"

criteria: Resource Scoring Algorithm section added to agent prompt
Scoring formula defined: base_quality * experience_match * topic_relevance
experience_match matrix provided for beginner/intermediate/advanced
topic_relevance scoring rules defined (exact/related/tangential)
Example calculation included showing score computation
Instruction to recommend top 3-5 resources sorted by score
verification: Read learn-planning-agent.md and verify algorithm section exists
Check that all three scoring factors are defined
Verify experience_match matrix has values for all 3 levels
Confirm example calculation is clear and correct

commit.message_template:
feat(learn): add resource scoring algorithm to agent prompt

tasks.title: Add learning path visualization to User Confirmation
tasks.description: Add ASCII-based learning path visualization to the User Confirmation phase in plan.md. Generate a visual DAG representation showing knowledge point levels and dependencies, along with the suggested learning order. This helps users understand the learning path before accepting the plan.

modification_points.target: Phase 6: User Confirmation section, before AskUserQuestion call
modification_points.change: Insert learning path visualization code using DAG topology

implementation: Read plan.md to locate Phase 6: User Confirmation section (around line 764)
Before the AskUserQuestion call, add console.log for Learning Path Visualization
Implement generateDagVisualization(dag) function that creates ASCII visualization
Use topological sort to group knowledge points into levels
Format output: 'Level 1: KP-1, KP-2' with arrows (↓) between levels
Add suggested learning order from validation.learning_order
Example output: 'Level 1: KP-1, KP-2 ↓ Level 2: KP-3, KP-4 ↓ Level 3: KP-5'
Add note: 'Visualization shows dependency-based learning sequence'
Reference implementation plan Phase 4.4

tasks.title: Test personalization with different profiles
tasks.description: Create test profiles with different experience levels (beginner, intermediate, advanced) and known topics. Run learn:plan with each profile to verify personalization features work correctly: high-proficiency topics are marked optional, difficulty distribution matches experience level, related experience is detected, resources are scored appropriately, and learning path visualization is clear.
implementation: Create test profile 1: beginner with empty known_topics
Create test profile 2: intermediate with known_topics [react (0.6), typescript (0.5)]
Create test profile 3: advanced with known_topics [react (0.9), typescript (0.8), nextjs (0.7)]
Run /learn:plan 'Master React Server Components' with profile 1 (beginner)
Verify: more easy KPs, tutorial resources prioritized, no optional KPs
Run /learn:plan 'Master React Server Components' with profile 2 (intermediate)
Verify: balanced difficulty, docs resources prioritized, related experience detected (React)
Run /learn:plan 'Master React Server Components' with profile 3 (advanced)
Verify: more hard KPs, React RSC basics marked optional (proficiency 0.9), advanced resources prioritized
Check Gap Analysis output shows related_experience and recommended_focus
Verify resource scoring in generated plan.json matches experience level
Check learning path visualization appears and is readable
